mission.title: Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
mission.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and operations of Kubernetes clusters in Azure. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated Azure services, but it may have limitations in terms of customization compared to self-managed Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install Azure CLI
steps.description:
The Azure CLI is required to interact with Azure services and manage your AKS cluster.

```bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

```

> For Debian-based systems

steps.title: Create an AKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new AKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31.0 --location eastus

```

> Replace 'myResourceGroup' and 'myAKSCluster' with your desired names.
commands: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31.0 --location eastus

steps.title: Configure kubectl Access
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the newly created AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

```

> This merges the AKS cluster credentials into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your AKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

az aks update --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --enable-cluster-autoscaler --min-count 1 --max-count 5

```

> Enables monitoring with Azure Monitor.
> Enables cluster autoscaler.
commands: az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete the AKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the AKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

az aks delete --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --yes --no-wait

```

> This will remove the cluster and orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade AKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Ensure you backup your workloads, then upgrade the AKS cluster to a new Kubernetes version.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version 1.31.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Get the current credentials.
> This upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are updated and in 'Ready' status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to get credentials'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the resource group or AKS cluster name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Double-check the resource group and cluster name in your command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient quota for requested resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your Azure subscription does not have enough quota for the requested resources.

**Fix:**
Request a quota increase through the Azure portal.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns no nodes
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the AKS cluster is not properly created or the credentials are not configured.

**Fix:**
Run 'az aks get-credentials' again and ensure the cluster is created successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node status is NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to issues with the node provisioning or network configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the AKS cluster events and logs for more details using 'kubectl describe nodes'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Azure regions and improved integration with Azure Monitor.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy monitoring solutions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure to update your monitoring configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31.0 --location eastus
az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Azure CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
